item.title: My Family Member Is Being Pressured to Donate a Kidney. Should I Say Something?
item.description: The recipient would be his stepfather’s colleague, whom he barely knows.

item.title: The Small-Business Owners Managing Whole Armies of A.I. Employees
item.description: When you turn A.I. agents loose on your finances, email and customers, what could possibly go wrong?

item.title: Should You Outsource Your Morning Routine to a Chatbot?
item.description: A.I. wants to help you start the day. Its suggestions? Drink coffee and get dressed.

item.title: From Cow-Milking Robots to Weed-Zapping Lasers, Farmers Are Embracing A.I.
item.description: We asked three farmers to tell us how new technology is revolutionizing the way they work.

item.title: Can I Force My Spouse to Do Karaoke?
item.description: A ruling on a duet-related dispute.

item.title: A Murdoch Builds His Own Media Empire. Is This ‘Succession’ or Secession?
item.description: James Murdoch says acquiring New York magazine and Vox has nothing to do with his father. But in some ways, it’s a tribute.

item.title: Raphael Warnock Says the Supreme Court Has Done ‘Violence’ to Democracy
item.description: The Democrat from Georgia on what he sees as the moral issues of our time.

item.title: My (Sort of) Ex Has Cancer. Is It Fair That She Expects Me to Take Care of Her?
item.description: Part of me recognizes that walking away is loathsome and something most people would judge harshly.

item.title: What Steven Spielberg Taught Me About Fear, Catharsis, and Being Human
item.description: Hollywood is struggling, but Spielberg insists that the big screen is still the best place to work out our collective dreams, joys and sorrows.

item.title: AIPAC Wants Democrats to Back Israel. Instead, They’re Turning on AIPAC.
item.description: Once the guardian of the bipartisan pro-Israel consensus, it is now a polarizing force in the party.

item.title: My Father Abused My Sister When We Were Kids. Should I Tell My Half Siblings?
item.description: I want them to grasp the differences between the two families he created.

item.title: 6 Takeaways From the Story of How the Epstein Files Paralyzed the White House
item.description: Senior officials clashed in a series of meetings as they struggled to manage a crisis over the president’s refusal to release the documents.

item.title: It’s Not Just Food. Our Entire World is ‘Ultraprocessed.’
item.description: The prefix has been used as a marketing tactic for decades. But when life feels extreme, it more often means something negative.

item.title: Inside the White House Freakout Over the Epstein Files
item.description: The president’s top advisers gathered in a series of Situation Room meetings as they struggled to contain a scandal engulfing Donald Trump himself.
